translated: Thank you so much.
How was it for you?
Well, since it was Tomari's first solo song,
I was really, really nervous.
Plus, it had been quite a while since I'd personally
performed at a Liella! live show.
So, I was actually pretty anxious about standing on stage alone to express Tomari's character.
But you know, from the very first day, during that initial 'clap-clap-clap' part,
even though I hadn't asked anyone to do it,
everyone joined in. I'd hoped they would, but
I didn't want to force it, so I didn't say anything.
In that moment, I felt this huge sense of relief.
I realized everyone was really watching and supporting me.
Even though I was standing on stage alone,
I felt like Tomari was with me and the audience was too. That feeling of not being alone
really gave me the courage to perform with confidence.
It's all thanks to everyone.
Thank you so much.
Man, it was just so good.
I absolutely loved it.
Yeah.
The way you did your expressions, and that sort of...
looking down from above, like a sidelong glance?
Saku,
as Tomari-chan,
was so good at that.
And that outfit suited you perfectly. It was so cute!
I have one favorite part—well, I love it all, but—
after the first chorus, when you sing 'Too Cute to Handle,'
on the... not the Oni... the big monitor?
You know that big monitor in the center?
Right after Saku says 'Too Cute to Handle' there,
she looks slightly to the side.
That face is seriously so cute. I mean, it's all cute,
every bit of it,
but while watching that, the cards... those cards,
they go 'whoosh' and
hide Tomari-chan behind them.
Up until then, we're all like, 'Ah, Tomari-chan!' but then
it's like she's gone,
like she's out of reach.
It just made my heart skip a beat.
I see. So you noticed that detail.
How should I put it... it's like,
there's a bit of mystery, but then there's that cute 'heart' feeling,
like the way you sang with hearts at the end of your lines.
And the dance used the stairs, right?
You had to move around, yet you kept your expressions and singing
perfectly in character as Tomari-chan the whole time.
It was truly amazing.
Aw, I'm so happy to hear that.
I definitely put a lot of thought into the expressions.
I knew I couldn't just be my usual self,
so I tried not to be too bubbly
and focused on keeping a cooler vibe.
Also, when we were learning the choreography,
they don't usually give specific instructions for your gaze.
Yeah, the dance teachers don't really tell you where to look.
So I thought about it myself, and
I felt like just staring straight ahead wouldn't be right.
I wanted to create moments where I'd look away, making people think, 'Wait, look at me!'
And then, 'Oh, she looked at me!'
I wanted to express that kind of back-and-forth teasing.
Whoa.
I thought it'd be good, so I intentionally added more moments
where I'd turn my face away. It was all calculated.
No way, that was calculated?!
Oh man, you've totally captured my heart. This is bad!
Also, for the choreography and expression changes,
I changed them slightly for each performance day. I hoped
that people who noticed would think, 'Oh, it's different today!'
I had that kind of thing going on too.
Actually, quite a lot of people noticed.
Oh?
They'd say things like, 'Wait, wasn't that different?'
when looking back at it,
or that the timing of the glances was different.
You really seemed so composed and effortless.
No, no, not at all.
But it was seriously so cute.
